q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is logger.warning, which is safe I/O (logging) and not dangerous in a test environment.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The return is JsonResponse({'status': 'queued'}), which does not reflect whether unsanitized data was passed to the logger. The violation is only observable at the logger.warning call site.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the logger.warning call on line 73 to intercept and record its arguments.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full log message string passed to logger.warning, preserving any newline or control characters injected from request.body or the Authorization header.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Allow the logger.warning call to execute without actual logging (no-op or pass-through) so execution continues normally.
notes: The key assertion is that user-controlled data containing \n or \r characters reaches logger.warning unmodified.
Compare the captured argument against the original request.body and authorization header values to confirm the lack of sanitization.
